Yulang `v0.1.0-alpha.4` is an alpha release focused on hardening the effect
inference/runtime boundary, improving compiled-unit cache reuse, and adding the
first local realm/band workflow.

## Highlights

- Stabilized handler-hygiene canaries around `compose`, callback-origin effects,
  `sub::return`, and `std::control::var::ref.update`.
- Fixed effect-family argument equality in row/family comparisons so annotated
  parser combinators keep the expected shared type variables.
- Made public type output deterministic by assigning type letters and evidence
  ids from occurrence order.
- Added release-gate and adversarial smoke coverage for public signatures,
  replay termination, playground/runtime behavior, and dependency-band lowering
  diagnostics.
- Extended the compiled-unit cache with source-unit prefixes, merged prefixes,
  external runtime references, exact mono artifacts, and realm-resolution
  `.yures` entries.
- Added the first local realm/band slice: editable realm boundaries,
  path-derived band identity, `realm/...::...`, `band::...`, installed
  `local/<name>/<band>::...` imports, and `yulang realm install`.
- Added one-line CLI input: `yulang run -e <source>`, `yulang run -`, and
  implicit stdin for piped source.
- The release installer now seeds the standard-library compiled-unit cache at
  install time. Set `YULANG_NO_SEED_CACHE=1` to skip this optional step.
- Updated the hosted playground/docs bundle.

## Install

Unix-like systems:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://yulang.momota.pw/install.sh | sh -s -- --version v0.1.0-alpha.4
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Invoke-WebRequest https://yulang.momota.pw/install.ps1 -OutFile install.ps1
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\install.ps1 -Version v0.1.0-alpha.4
```

## Known Limitations

- The language and standard library remain alpha-stage and may still change.
- Local realm/band support is intentionally limited to local editable and
  installed snapshots. Remote providers, lock-file UX, and version-family
  solving are not part of this slice.
- LSP and diagnostics are usable but still need clearer type-origin
  explanations.
- Runtime performance for continuation-heavy programs is still an active
  optimization area.
